title: Animal trade: the uncertain destiny of animals imported into Armenia and the tacit consent of the state
content: Between 2017 and 2022, nearly 4,000 wild animals were imported into Armenia from various parts of the world. The Ministry of Environment and other authorities lack comprehensive information about imported and exported animals. In 2021, 45 giraffes were imported into Armenia; one perished during transfer and another died shortly after arriving, with the fate of the remaining giraffes currently unknown.

title: Suspect Donations and Unidentified Sources of the Ruling Civil Contract's Election Campaign Finances
content: Investigation into suspicious transactions associated with the Civil Contract party's 2023 Yerevan Council of Elders election campaign. A citizen's ID card and signature were exploited to channel funds of unverified origin into the party's account. The party did not disclose the identities of 987 donors who contributed 506,532,950 AMD despite repeated media requests. Infocom examined and identified nearly all individuals who contributed donations exceeding 1 million AMD.

title: Foreign Minister of Armenia Ararat Mirzoyan received the Secretary General of BSEC Lazar Comanescu
content: Foreign Minister of Armenia Ararat Mirzoyan received the Secretary General of the Organization of the Black Sea Economic Cooperation (BSEC) Lazar Comanescu. The interlocutors noted the 30th anniversary of BSEC and emphasized further development, with Mirzoyan stressing the importance of expanding sectoral cooperation.

title: Vahram Dumanyan appointed as an adviser to the President of Armenia
content: Vahram Dumanyan, former Minister of Education, Science, Culture and Sports of the Republic of Armenia, was appointed adviser to the President of Armenia. He had submitted an application for dismissal from the position of minister on December 12, 2022. He was appointed to the position on November 23, 2020, and before that held the position of dean of the Faculty of Informatics and Applied Mathematics of YSU. Zhanna Andreasyan was appointed the new minister on December 13, 2022.

title: Pashinyan sent a congratulatory message to Zelensky on the New Year and Christmas holidays
content: Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan sent a congratulatory message to Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on the occasion of the New Year and Christmas holidays.

title: Azerbaijan intends to close the Lachin Corridor - Alen Simonyan
content: Coverage of the Azerbaijani blockade of the Lachin Corridor. The 'Regional Development' NGO linked to the Azerbaijani government was behind the so-called environmental protest blocking the corridor. The European Parliament adopted a resolution deploring the severe humanitarian crisis caused by the blockade and condemning the inactivity of Russian peacekeepers.

title: It's unacceptable for us the more and more visible practice of the Russian peacekeeping troops to become a silent witness to the depopulation of Nagorno Karabakh - Pashinyan
content: PM Pashinyan stated that if Russia was unable to ensure stability and security in Nagorno-Karabakh, it should organize a discussion in the UN Security Council and endow the Russian peacekeeping force with a UN mandate or raise the issue of sending additional multinational peacekeepers. Pashinyan declared it unacceptable that Russian peacekeeping troops were becoming a silent witness to the depopulation of the Nagorno-Karabakh region.

title: On one hand, Aliyev keeps stating that they're ready to talk with NK Armenians, on the other hand, they attack - Edmon Marukyan
content: RA Ambassador-at-Large Edmon Marukyan referred to the Armenian-Azerbaijani conflict, discussing Azerbaijan's ceasefire violations in Nagorno-Karabakh and the contradiction between Aliyev's stated willingness to talk with NK Armenians and ongoing military attacks.

title: There will be some actions, there will be answers - Zakharova announced that Russian peacekeepers will respond to the appearance of representatives of the EU mission
content: Maria Zakharova, representative of the Russian Foreign Ministry, announced that Russian peacekeepers will respond to the appearance of representatives of the EU mission in the border regions of Armenia. RA President Vahagn Khachaturyan welcomed the deployment of the EU's long-term 100-person monitoring mission along the Armenian border with Azerbaijan.

title: Opposition has no final decision yet over nominating candidate for President of Armenia
content: The opposition has not yet made a final decision regarding nominating a candidate for the President of Armenia. Coverage of political dynamics in Armenia surrounding the presidential nomination process.

title: Peace agenda has broad public consensus, says MP Lena Nazaryan
content: MP Lena Nazaryan stated that the peace agenda has broad public consensus in Armenia. Coverage of the ruling party's position on peace negotiations.

title: Our goal is to prevent any new concessions at any cost - Ishkhan Saghatelyan
content: Opposition figure Ishkhan Saghatelyan stated that the goal is to prevent any new concessions at any cost, reflecting the opposition's stance on peace negotiations with Azerbaijan.

title: Armenia-Turkey: ban on direct air cargo lifted in January 2024
content: The ban on direct air cargo between Turkey and Armenia was lifted in January 2024 as part of ongoing normalization efforts. Officials expressed hope to fully open the common border which was unilaterally closed by Turkey in the early 1990s.

title: Fact-check: Russian plane from Syria via Erebuni airport and mercenary transfer claims
content: Fact-check: To transfer mercenaries from Latakia or another part of Syria, it is not necessary to do it through Armenia. The fact that the Russian RA-85041 plane arrived from Syria to the Armenian Erebuni airport, and from there flew to the Russian Federation, does not prove the fact of transferring mercenaries on that plane.

title: Hayantar Employee Salaries to Increase by 20-70,000 AMD
content: From March 2021, the salaries of employees of Hayantar SNCO will increase by 20-70,000 AMD, including salaries of forest rangers and forest managers increasing by 20,000 AMD. The decision was made possible through cost optimization and savings from the Ministry of Environment.

title: The construction of 9 houses almost completed in Shurnukh
content: The Chairman of the Urban Development Committee, Armen Ghularyan, and the Head of the Urban Planning Implementation Department, Arsen Mirzoyan, paid a working visit to the Syunik region to supervise the construction of a new district in the Shurnukh settlement. The construction works of 9 houses in the district, including 13 residential houses, are almost completed, and 4 are in progress. A new road in the district was built, and the construction works of engineering infrastructures and fences were started. The construction works of the new district in the Shurnukh settlement of the expanded community of Goris started on April 26, 2021, with the project costing around 1.2 billion AMD.

title: Nikol Pashinyan spoke at the UN about the return of Armenian POWs, unblocking roads and establishing peace in the region
content: Coverage of PM Pashinyan's speech at the UN General Assembly, addressing the situation in the South Caucasus, the 2020 Nagorno-Karabakh aggression, and the displacement of tens of thousands of residents.

title: Armenian POWs and civilian hostages held in Azerbaijan must be repatriated as soon as possible. Mirzoyan to the new Russian co-chair of the MG
content: Article about Armenian POWs and civilian hostages held in Azerbaijan, covering sections like the Artsakh conflict and the 44-day war chronology.

title: Meeting between President Vahagn Khachaturyan and President of Georgia Salome Zourabichvili
content: A meeting between President Vahagn Khachaturyan and President of Georgia Salome Zourabichvili took place at the Orbeliani Palace in Georgia.

title: Professor Armen Charchyan detained by police
content: Article about the detention of Professor Armen Charchyan by police.

title: The Fair Armenia party issues an official statement on parliamentary elections
content: A statement from the Fair Armenia party on the results of snap parliamentary elections, describing them as a key message to understand the essence of human relations during political activity.

title: Snap parliamentary elections 2021: election campaign day 4
content: Coverage of the fourth day of the election campaign for the 2021 snap parliamentary elections in Armenia.

title: Daily News Summary [12.03.2022]
content: Tensions around Nagorno-Karabakh inching up, as world's focus is centered on Ukraine.

title: Pashinyan congratulated Netanyahu on his reappointment as Israel's Prime Minister
content: An article about PM Pashinyan congratulating Benjamin Netanyahu on his reappointment as Israel's Prime Minister and the formation of a new government.

title: Masis City Landfill Fire extinguished after nine days
content: A report about firefighting operations at the Masis city landfill that lasted nine days before being concluded.

title: Criminal Case of egg attack on MPs Andranik Kocharyan and Sisak Gabrielyan sent to court
content: Coverage of a criminal case involving a hooliganism incident against MPs Andranik Kocharyan and Sisak Gabrielyan, sent to court in December 2022.

title: Serzh Sargsyan campaign speech during I Have Honor alliance campaign
content: An article about former President Serzh Sargsyan's speech during the I Have Honor alliance campaign for the snap parliamentary elections.

title: Archbishop Navasard Kchjoyan summoned to RA Prosecutor General's office
content: An article about Archbishop Navasard Kchjoyan being summoned to the RA Prosecutor General's office regarding an investigation into illegally obtained property.

title: PM Pashinyan's address to the Armenian people regarding the war launched by Turkey and Azerbaijan against Artsakh
content: Coverage of PM Pashinyan's appeal to the Armenian people regarding the war launched by Turkey and Azerbaijan against Artsakh. He said: We must turn our grief into rage, our fears into determination, our doubts into action.

title: Robert Kocharyan's criticism of government's security policies at UN
content: Coverage of former President Robert Kocharyan's criticism of the government's security policies, calling the PM's UN speech a farewell speech to the era of peace.

title: Infocom Article on Armenia Central Bank raising refinancing rate
content: Article referencing the Armenia Central Bank raising its refinancing rate, with related economic policy coverage.

title: Impact of Second Nagorno-Karabakh War on Armenian society
content: Coverage related to the impact of the Second Nagorno-Karabakh War on Armenian society, noting that the conflict cost over 4,000 Armenian lives and plunged the country into a prolonged political crisis.

title: Mher Grigoryan received EAEU Minister Sergey Glazyev to discuss EAEU cooperation
content: Deputy Prime Minister Mher Grigoryan received EAEU Minister Sergey Glazyev to discuss EAEU cooperation and economic integration matters.
